name: motheme
documentation:
# Marimo Custom Themes > personalize your experience with > [marimo](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo) ## Theme Gallery ### coldme
### nord
### mininini
### wigwam
## Get Started ```bash # Install motheme CLI tool pip install motheme # Help messages motheme # Initialize themes motheme update # List available themes motheme themes # Apply a theme to specific files motheme apply coldme notebook1.py notebook2.py # Or, apply theme recursively in a directory motheme apply -r coldme ./ ``` > [!NOTE] > > Please note that some parts of the Marimo notebook are not fully exposed for > customization at this time. This includes side panels and cell editors > [!WARNING] > > You may want to run `motheme clear -r ./` before sharing or uploading your > notebooks because the field `css_file` in `marimo.App()` may leak your > private data. ## Usage - **Requirements**: Ensure you are using Marimo version **0.9.14** or higher. It is recommended to keep your Marimo version up-to-date for the best experience. - **Light and Dark Mode Support**: All themes support both light and dark modes and will switch automatically based on your notebook's current theme settings. ## Contributing To contribute your own themes, please follow these guidelines: - **Refer to Default Arguments**: All available arguments are listed in [`default.css`](themes/default/default.css), which serves as a reference for writing your themes. You can experiment with other arguments, but please note that stability cannot be guaranteed with untested changes. - **Light and Dark Themes**: Implement both light and dark themes using the light-dark syntax as demonstrated in `default.css`. If you choose not to implement a theme for a specific mode, name your theme as `xxx_light` or `xxx_dark` and use the default values for the respective mode from `default.css`. - **Folder Structure**: After finishing your CSS file, create a new folder inside `themes` with the name of your theme. Within this folder, upload the following: - The CSS file - Preview images - An optional `README.md` file to illustrate your design - **Design Using Sample**: You can design your theme using the [`sample.py`](sample.py) file provided in the repository. This file helps visualize how your theme will look in the Marimo notebook.

modules.name: motheme.clear_theme
documentation: Clear theme from marimo notebooks.

functions.name: motheme.clear_theme.clean_app_line
documentation:
Remove css_file parameter and cleaning up punctuation.

Args:
    line: The line containing marimo.App() call.

Returns:
    Cleaned line with css_file parameter removed and punctuation fixed

functions.name: motheme.clear_theme.process_file
documentation:
Process a single file to remove theme settings.

Args:
    file_name: Path to the file to process

Returns:
    Tuple of (success, modified_content)
    - success: True if theme was cleared, False if no theme found
    - modified_content: List of lines with theme removed

functions.name: motheme.clear_theme.clear_theme
documentation:
Remove theme settings from specified notebook files.

Args:
    files: List of Marimo notebook files to modify

modules.name: motheme.util
documentation: Utility functions.

functions.name: motheme.util.validate_theme_exists
documentation: Validate theme exists and return its path.

functions.name: motheme.util.get_themes_dir
documentation: Get the themes directory path.

functions.name: motheme.util.is_marimo_file
documentation:
Check if a file is a Marimo notebook.

A file is considered a Marimo notebook if it:
1. Has .py extension
2. Has an exact 'import marimo' line
3. Creates a marimo.App instance
4. Contains at least one @app.cell decorator

modules.name: motheme.apply_theme
documentation: Apply a Marimo theme to specified notebook files.

functions.name: motheme.apply_theme.modify_app_line
documentation: Modify a marimo.App line to include or update the css_file parameter.

functions.name: motheme.apply_theme.process_file
documentation: Process a single file and return (success, new_content).

functions.name: motheme.apply_theme.apply_theme
documentation:
Apply a Marimo theme to specified notebook files.

```
param theme_name

Name of the theme to apply

param files

List of Marimo notebook files to modify
```

modules.name: motheme
documentation: Marimo theme manager.

modules.name: motheme.list_themes
documentation: List available themes.

functions.name: motheme.list_themes.list_themes
documentation: List available themes.

modules.name: motheme.current_theme
documentation: Show current theme of marimo notebooks.

functions.name: motheme.current_theme.extract_theme_name
documentation:
Extract theme name from marimo.App line.

Args:
    line: The line containing marimo.App() call

Returns:
    Theme name if found, None otherwise

functions.name: motheme.current_theme.current_theme
documentation:
Show currently applied themes for specified notebook files.

Args:
    files: List of Marimo notebook files to check

modules.name: motheme.cli
documentation: CLI for motheme.

functions.name: motheme.cli.update
documentation: Update Marimo themes from GitHub repository.

functions.name: motheme.cli.themes
documentation: List available Marimo themes.

functions.name: motheme.cli._expand_files
documentation:
Expand file paths, optionally recursively for directories.
Only includes valid Marimo notebook files.

Args:
    files: Tuple of file/directory paths
    recursive: If True, recursively search directories for Python files

Returns:
    List of expanded file paths that are Marimo notebooks

functions.name: motheme.cli._check_files_provided
documentation:
Check if files were provided and print error message if not.

Args:
    action_description: Description of the action being performed
    files: Tuple of file/directory paths

Returns:
    bool: True if files were provided, False otherwise

functions.name: motheme.cli.apply
documentation: Apply a Marimo theme to specified notebook files.

functions.name: motheme.cli.clear
documentation: Remove theme settings from specified notebook files.

functions.name: motheme.cli.current
documentation: Show currently applied themes for specified notebook files.

functions.name: motheme.cli.main
documentation: CLI entry point.

modules.name: motheme.app_parser
documentation: Parser for marimo.App declarations in notebook files.

functions.name: motheme.app_parser.find_app_block
documentation:
Find and extract the marimo.App block from file content.

Returns None if no App block is found.

functions.name: motheme.app_parser.update_file_content
documentation: Replace the App block in the file content with new content.

classes.name: motheme.app_parser.AppBlock
documentation: Represents a marimo.App block in a notebook file.

modules.name: motheme.theme_downloader
documentation: Module for downloading Marimo themes from a GitHub repository.

documentation: Convert GitHub repo URL to API URL.

functions.name: motheme.theme_downloader._download_theme
documentation: Download a single theme CSS file.

functions.name: motheme.theme_downloader.download_themes
documentation:
Download Marimo themes CSS files from GitHub repository.

Args:
    repo_url (str): GitHub repository URL

Returns:
    Path: Local directory where themes are stored
